name: Irving Ashby
abstract: Irving Conrad Ashby (December 29, 1920 – April 22, 1987) was an American jazz guitarist. Ashby was born in  Somerville, Massachusetts and started playing guitar when he was nine.

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio
Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio

works.t: No No Nanette: Tea for Two

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder Part 1
about_quotes.evidence: Hamp’s band was some band... Irving Ashby, who later was with Nat King Cole, the guitarist. And speaking of, we had a saxophone player, Ray Perry... It was an interesting band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder Part 1 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Thompson
about_quotes.evidence: But the basic group was made up of... Irving Ashby on guitar, and myself on piano.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: And she took Arnold Ross and Irving Ashby and myself. We were her trio.

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Incidentally, Irving Ashby is the man we're thinking about. Irving Ashby is the guitar player, he had a tendency to drink. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer lists Irving Ashby in a note as being in the group, but the interviewee does not mention him by name or describe a personal interaction.

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: the rhythm section ended up being Sir Charles on piano, Vernon Alley on bass, and Irving Ashby... I did all the singing with Hamp's band
